title: Handoff promotion requires a provenance-bound additive authorization record

observed: M-001 is completed and includes a handoff, while no active continuity_mining queue item exists.
CM-0010 already separates completed state, proposed handoff, authorization, execution, and verification.
The repository previously lacked a canonical machine-readable record for promoting a handoff into newly owned work.
inferred: A promotion record must preserve the immutable source item and bind the exact handoff content before assigning a new owner.
Promotion is a state transition between linked records, not mutation of the completed source record.
proposed: Use operations/continuity/handoff-promotion.schema.json for every future handoff-derived authorization.
Require source blob identity, handoff content digest, new queue identity, owner, authority basis, acceptance state, and privacy boundary.
superseded: Representing handoff-derived work only as prose in a completed queue item's handoff field.
Reopening or rewriting a completed queue item to imply new ownership.
unresolved: No runtime validator currently enforces this JSON Schema.
The canonical ACTIVE_QUEUE format does not yet link promoted work back to a promotion_id.
No authorized promotion instance has yet been accepted by a receiving team.

durable_memory.rule: A handoff becomes executable only through a new additive record that binds source history, exact handoff content, new ownership, explicit authority, and acceptance state.
durable_memory.fail_closed: Absent a schema-valid authorization with acceptance_status=authorized, retain the handoff as proposed and do not claim active execution.

falsification_or_review_route: Reject or revise this rule if a canonical repository governance document explicitly authorizes execution through a handoff field alone while preserving equivalent source identity, owner acceptance, and state provenance.
